xai.note: Legacy cap — news_monitor.py archived 2026-04-23. Kept for any future xAI-using daemons.

perplexity.note: Legacy cap — news_monitor.py archived 2026-04-23 (Apr 23 $100 incident). NEVER use Perplexity for code (CLAUDE.md rule).

anthropic.note: Hard-coded in shared_gates.py _ANTHROPIC_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Caps Haiku NC scans + ad-hoc reasoning calls. 500/day = ~$1.50/day max. Cache + 60s error cooldown protect within budget.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only deep-verify auto-call uses it; cap is plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.gemini:
**Reasoning:** 20 timers exist but 0 projected calls. The analyzer missed dynamic calls, rendering the "LOW" risk falsely secure. 

**(a) Runaway loop:** Anthropic's 60s cooldown only protects against *errors*. A logical loop on *successful* ad-hoc responses drains the 500-call cap instantly.
**(b) Missing caps:** Hard USD/token caps are missing. Request-count caps do not limit payload sizes.
**(c) 2x Blow-up:** Anthropic. If token volume doubles, the "$1.50/day max" assumption fails catastrophically. 500 heavy-context calls cost hundreds, not $1.50.
**(d) Silent burn:** Anthropic "ad-hoc reasoning" easily evades static schedule detection.

**If wrong forecast:** If the 20 timers are purely local non-API tasks, this audit needlessly delays deployment. But trusting the $0 projection risks t
ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Audit verdict: "LOW" is misleading — this is observability-blind, not safe.**

(a) **Runaway loops:** 20 timers discovered, 0 projected calls — the auditor isn't actually attributing schedules to services. Any timer calling an LLM in a retry/except loop (esp. Anthropic Haiku scans) can spin freely below the 500/day cap until a bug fans it out.

(b) **Missing caps:** No per-minute/hour caps anywhere — only daily. A burst at 03:00 can exhaust budget before an operator wakes. No global USD ceiling.

(c) **2× blow-up:** Anthropic doubles to ~$3/day (tolerable). Perplexity at 2× cap = repeat of the Apr 23 $100 incident — its $/call is the killer.

(d) **Most likely silent burn:** **Anthropic Haiku NC scans** — high cap, cheap-per-call, runs headless, cache failures invisible.
ai_consensus.grok:
**Blow-up risks:**

(a) Runaway loops: 20 timers discovered but zero calls projected suggests scanner missed bot error-retry paths. Anthropic/Gemini loops without 60s cooldown could exhaust 500/100 daily caps in minutes.

(b) Missing caps: XAI & Perplexity only have legacy news_monitor.py caps (archived Apr 23 after $100 Perplexity incident). New trading bot code bypasses them entirely.

(c) 2x rate: Anthropic doubles to ~$3/day (still under hard cap); others negligible.

(d) Most stealth: **Perplexity** — explicitly banned for code, legacy cap ignored, no active monitoring or findings. Will burn silently. 

Overall: projected 0 is false negative. Add bot-specific gates before launch.
